title: potentilla procumbens var. humilis
intro: Potentilla procumbens var. humilis, also known as dwarf potentilla or low potentilla, is a low-growing, spreading plant native to eastern North America, characterized by its small, pink flowers and trifoliate leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Potentilla procumbens var. humilis are believed to provide various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-cancer, and cardiovascular-protective effects. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to support immune function and skin health.
